content.text:
<user_query>
generate an app that parces buyers from LinkedIn and stores the data as excel format using ampify
</user_query>

Building an AWS Amplify app that stores buyer leads and exports to Excel. We won't implement LinkedIn scraping (violates LinkedIn's Terms of Service and is unreliable). We'll support manual entry, CSV import, and parsing pasted LinkedIn profile URLs or snippet text the user provides.

[REDACTED]

Creating a new project and scaffolding a React + Amplify Gen 2 app with a Buyer model, CSV/paste import, and Excel export.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Scaffold Vite React TypeScript app in project dir

input.command: source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh 2>/dev/null; command -v npm || which npm; echo PATH=$PATH

content.text:
Scaffolding the project manually because npm isn't available in this environment. Creating the full Amplify Gen 2 + React + Vite structure.

[REDACTED]

input.contents:
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "lib": ["ES2022", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "module": "ESNext",
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "strict": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true
  },
  "include": ["src", "amplify"]
}

content.text:
Fixing Amplify UI `Authenticator` usage, buyer types, and handling optional `amplify_outputs.json` for clones.

[REDACTED]

Using a committed fallback and ignoring sandbox-generated `amplify_outputs.json` so we don't commit secrets.

[REDACTED]

input.new_string:
Buyer leads
